nodes.name: 鸻形目
nodes.summary: 鸻形目（Charadriiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联310种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、短腿鸻、白鞘嘴鸥等310种鸟类。

nodes.name: Palmgrove National Park
nodes.summary: Palmgrove is a national park in south-central Queensland, Australia.

nodes.name: 雨林
nodes.summary: 雨林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联99种鸟类，包括叢塚雉、鳳冠孔雀雉、Amboyna cuckoo-dove等99种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联694种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等694种鸟类。

nodes.name: 三趾鹑科
nodes.summary: 三趾鹑科（Turnicidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联12种鸟类，如Sumba buttonquail、Black-rumped buttonquail、Fynbos buttonquail等12种鸟类。

nodes.name: 重点鸟区
nodes.summary: 重点鸟区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括Black-breasted buttonquail、Meratus white-eye、Meratus blue flycatcher等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Black-breasted buttonquail
nodes.summary: Black-breasted buttonquail（Black-breasted buttonquail，学名 Turnix melanogaster），分类上属于鸻形目，并归入三趾鹑科，在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，已关联3个分布地点节点。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The black-breasted buttonquail is usually found in rainforests, foraging on the ground for invertebrates in large areas of thick leaf litter

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | last5=Murray | title=Distribution of Black-breasted Button-quail Turnix melanogaster in the Great Sandy Region, Queensland and associations with vegetation communities | journal= Emu – Austral Ornithology | doi=10.1080/01584197.2022.2047733 | date= March 2022| volume= 122 | issue= 1 | pages= 71–76| bibcode= 2022EmuAO.122...71W | s2cid= 247534104 }} It is found in Palmgrove National Park, which has been identified by BirdLife International as an Important Bird Area for the species. The black-breasted buttonquail was once populous on Inskip Point, with the area a destination for birdwatchers wanting to see this species

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: | last5=Murray | title=Distribution of Black-breasted Button-quail Turnix melanogaster in the Great Sandy Region, Queensland and associations with vegetation communities | journal= Emu – Austral Ornithology | doi=10.1080/01584197.2022.2047733 | date= March 2022| volume= 122 | issue= 1 | pages= 71–76| bibcode= 2022EmuAO.122...71W | s2cid= 247534104 }} It is found in Palmgrove National Park, which has been identified by BirdLife International as an Important Bird Area for the species. The black-breasted buttonquail was once populous on Inskip Point, with the area a destination for birdwatchers wanting to see this species
